data.title: Hanshow partners with University of Cambridge for research on Augmented RFID, aiming to revolutionize retail tech.
data.description: A global leader in digital retail technology, Hanshow, has initiated a multi-year research collaboration with the University of Cambridge in London. This partnership aims to create advanced Augmented RFID systems using distributed hardware architectures. By combining Cambridge's proficiency in ultra-low-power sensing and communication with Hanshow's large-scale deployment capabilities, the goal is to establish a new technological standard for the retail industry worldwide. This venture signifies a significant step forward in the sector's innovation efforts and underscores the potential for groundbreaking advancements in the near future.

data.title: Beer market to reach USD 999 billion by 2030, increase from USD 804.65 billion in 2025, driven by premiumization.
data.description: Hyderabad, India - In 2025, the beer market is valued at USD 804.65 billion and is forecasted to increase to USD 998.98 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 4.42%, as per Mordor Intelligence. This growth is propelled by premium product lines and a variety of options available in the market.

data.title: American Bitcoin Corp (NASDAQ: ABTC) stock down over 60% in a month, but analyst initiated 'Buy' rating, projecting $4 price target (106% upside).
data.description: Recently, American Bitcoin Corp. (NASDAQ: ABTC) has been experiencing significant sell-offs, but a Roth Capital analyst has predicted a potential increase of over 100% for the stock. On Wednesday, Roth Capital's analyst Darren Aftahi initiated coverage on ABTC with a "Buy" rating and set a price target of $4, indicating a 106% upside from the closing price on that day. Aftahi has a track record of being correct 47% of the time. Notably, Roth Capital is linked to a special-purpose acquisition company named Colombier Acquisition Corp. III, where Donald Trump Jr. serves as a director, according to Reuters. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: QualityKiosk is the first digital assurance provider to adopt AI-native technology in partnership with Computer by DevRev.
data.description: In Princeton, New Jersey, QualityKiosk has made history as the first prominent digital assurance provider to fully embrace AI technology with Computer by DevRev. This move signifies a significant advancement in the industry, setting QualityKiosk apart from its competitors. The integration of AI into its services positions QualityKiosk at the forefront of innovation, demonstrating its commitment to leveraging cutting-edge technology. This milestone marks a new era for the company and showcases its dedication to enhancing the quality of its offerings.

data.title: U.S. stock futures slip post rate cut announcement by Fed. Dow Jones -0.16%, S&P 500 -0.53%, NASDAQ 100 -0.72%. Oracle's shares drop by 11.01% after Q2 results, while Broadcom falls 1.98% pre-earnings. Fed's QE plan and market forecast noted. Bond yields at 10-year: 4.14% and two-year: 3.53%. Quantitative easing includes purchasing $40 billion in Treasury bills/month.
data.description: U.S. stock futures fell after a Federal Reserve rate cut, with benchmark indices lower. The Fed cut rates to 3.5%–3.75%, continuing a third consecutive reduction and announcing $40 billion monthly Treasury bill purchases. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.14% and the two-year bond stood at 3.53%. The market projects an 80.1% chance of unchanged rates. Futures showed a decline: Dow Jones (-0.16%), S&P 500 (-0.53%), Nasdaq 100 (-0.72%), Russell 2000 (-0.01%). ETFs SPY and QQQ followed suit, with SPY at $683.99 (-0.52%) and QQQ at $623.08 (-0.72%). Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) fell 11.01% post-mixed Q2 results, while Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) decreased 1.98% before earnings; analysts anticipate $1.86 EPS and $17.1491 billion revenue for the quarter. AVGO maintained a stronger price trend. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Former Fed economist cautions that more rate cuts could indicate economic weakness, warns against focusing on immediate effects. Powell's 'last cut' potentially at risk per Claudia Sahm.
data.description: A cautionary message has been delivered by Claudia Sahm, a former economist at the Federal Reserve, regarding the potential implications of the Fed's forthcoming rate cuts. Sahm, a well-known figure in the financial sphere, has raised concerns that these rate cuts could signify underlying issues in the economy. Emphasizing the importance of looking beyond the immediate impacts of the cuts, she highlighted the fragility of the job market that the Fed is working to safeguard. Sahm expressed apprehension that further rate reductions by the Jerome Powell-led Fed could indicate economic weakness, advising caution and stating, "be careful what you wish for." She also advised against placing too much reliance on initial jobless claims as a sole indicator of economic health. For further details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cognizant opens AI Lab and Moment™ Studio in Bengaluru to accelerate enterprise transformation.
data.description: A new innovation hub in BENGALURU, India, opened by Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) on Dec. 11, 2025, combines AI research and user experience to drive enterprise transformation. This hub includes the India Artificial Intelligence (AI) Lab and a Cognizant MomentTM Studio. This initiative aims to accelerate the advancement of AI technologies while focusing on enhancing user experiences.

data.title: Coalition for Prediction Markets formed by Kalshi, Crypto.com, Robinhood, Coinbase, and Underdog for fair and open access.
data.description: A new coalition has been formed by industry leaders Kalshi and Crypto.com, along with Coinbase, Robinhood, and Underdog, in Washington on December 11, 2025. This alliance aims to enhance prediction capabilities and includes important players in the cryptocurrency and financial sectors. This collective effort underscores a significant step in the industry involving key companies, with the primary focus on advancing predictive analytics and fostering collaboration.

data.title: XRP fees decrease by 89%, leading to potential price drop below $1.75 with a risk of 15% to $1.73 due to reduced demand.
data.description: The price of XRP faced a potential 15% decline, dropping to a low of $1.73. This drop was influenced by decreasing transaction fees, decreased interest from speculators, and a weakening technical foundation.

data.title: Int'l firms face new trade challenges in US tariff year, turning to geopolitics for risk management - Willis Index helps prepare.
data.description: In December 11, 2025, the global trade environment experienced drastic changes due to the impact of U.S. tariff agreements. This year was marked by significant shifts in geopolitical dynamics, emphasizing the importance of national security alignments for businesses operating on a global scale. To navigate these evolving risks and ensure long-term resilience, companies are increasingly focusing on effective risk management strategies. The most recent Political Risk Index, provided by Willis (a WTW business listed on NASDAQ as WTW), serves as a tool to differentiate meaningful risk indicators from irrelevant background noise. It equips multinational corporations with valuable insights to brace themselves for the forthcoming era of tariff-driven geopolitics.
